commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Slack `message search` support through Slack Web API `search.messages`, updates message dispatch/CLI/docs, and adds focused Slack and Discord tests.

PR surface: Source +236, Tests +384, Docs +3. Total +623 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report with a current-main failure to reproduce. The relevant proof is whether the new Slack search path works in a real workspace after the patch.
commands.review_followup: Needs human handling for conflict resolution, contributor real-behavior proof, and the maintainer choice between this narrow branch and the broader Slack action-parity PR.

target.title: feat(slack): add message search support via search.messages API
target.body:
## Summary
- add Slack message search support backed by `search.messages`
- expose the search action when an enabled Slack account has a user token
- extend CLI/search dispatch and add focused Slack tests

## Notes
- requires a Slack User Token (`xoxp-`) with `search:read` scope
- rebuilt cleanly from the closed #58143 on top of current upstream main

## Verification
- reviewed and revised before commit
- passed focused tests:
  - `extensions/slack/src/message-actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack message search routing still uses user-token-capable accounts and dispatches the search action after the branch was brought current with main.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev workspace on Linux/WSL2 after merging current origin/main into this PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/actions.search.test.ts extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw workspace:
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-slack.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.53s
- Observed result after fix: The Slack search action and message-action dispatch coverage passed on the refreshed branch head.
- What was not tested: A real Slack workspace search.messages API call was not executed because it would require a live user token and workspace data access.
